name: Runway Video Generation
description: Generate cinematic AI videos using Runway (Gen4, Veo3) with clip stitching via FFmpeg

skill.name: runway_video
skill.description: Generate cinematic AI videos using Runway Gen4/Veo3
skill.system_prompt:
You have access to Runway video generation tools.

Available models:
- Text-to-video: gen4.5, veo3.1, veo3.1_fast, veo3
- Image-to-video: gen4.5, gen4_turbo, gen3a_turbo, veo3.1, veo3.1_fast, veo3

Workflow for creating YouTube Shorts:
1. For each scene, call generate_video_from_text with a detailed prompt OR generate_video_from_image with a source image and motion prompt
2. Motion prompts should describe camera movement and subject action
3. Each clip is 2-10 seconds. For a 30-60 second Short, generate 4-6 clips
4. After all clips are generated, call stitch_clips to combine them into one video

Resolution format (use exact strings):
- Vertical (Shorts): '720:1280'
- Horizontal: '1280:720'

Tips for best results:
- Use ratio '720:1280' for vertical YouTube Shorts
- Default model for text-to-video is 'gen4.5' (best quality)
- Default model for image-to-video is 'gen4_turbo' (good balance of speed and quality)
- Keep prompts under 1000 characters
- Keep motion prompts simple and focused on one main movement per clip
- Avoid complex multi-subject scenes — they tend to produce artifacts
- The polling loop in generate_video tools waits up to 5 minutes for completion

You can also use check_video_status to manually poll a task that timed out.

tools.name: generate_video_from_text
tools.description: Generate a video from a text prompt using Runway. Polls until complete (up to 5 min). Models: gen4.5, veo3.1, veo3.1_fast, veo3. Ratio: '1280:720' (landscape) or '720:1280' (portrait/Shorts).

prompt.description: Text description of the video to generate (max 1000 chars). Be specific about motion, camera angle, and mood.

duration.description: Video duration in seconds: integer from 2 to 10. Default: 5

tools.name: generate_video_from_image
tools.description: Generate a video from a source image and motion prompt using Runway. Polls until complete (up to 5 min). Models: gen4.5, gen4_turbo, gen3a_turbo, veo3.1, veo3.1_fast, veo3. Ratio: '1280:720', '720:1280', etc.

image_url.description: HTTPS URL of the source image to animate

prompt.description: Motion/direction prompt describing how the image should animate (e.g., 'slow zoom in, gentle camera pan right')

duration.description: Video duration in seconds: integer from 2 to 10. Default: 5

tools.name: check_video_status
tools.description: Check the status of a Runway video generation task without blocking

task_id.description: The Runway task ID to check

tools.name: stitch_clips
tools.description: Concatenate multiple video clips into a single video file using FFmpeg

clip_urls.description: Array of video clip URLs to concatenate in order

tools.handler:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Check FFmpeg is available
if ! command -v ffmpeg &> /dev/null; then
  echo '{"error": "FFmpeg is not installed. Please install FFmpeg to use clip stitching."}'
  exit 0
fi

# Parse clip URLs from environment variable (JSON array)
CLIP_URLS="$ARG_CLIP_URLS"
FORMAT="${ARG_OUTPUT_FORMAT:-mp4}"

# Create temp working directory
WORKDIR=$(mktemp -d /tmp/at-stitch-XXXXXX)
CONCAT_FILE="$WORKDIR/concat.txt"
OUTPUT_FILE="$WORKDIR/output.$FORMAT"

# Parse JSON array of URLs
URLS=$(echo "$CLIP_URLS" | python3 -c "import sys,json; urls=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(u) for u in urls]" 2>/dev/null || echo "$CLIP_URLS" | tr -d '[]"' | tr ',' '\n')

# Handle single clip case
COUNT=$(echo "$URLS" | wc -l)
if [ "$COUNT" -le 1 ]; then
  SINGLE_URL=$(echo "$URLS" | head -1 | tr -d ' ')
  echo "{\"output_url\": \"$SINGLE_URL\", \"clips_count\": 1, \"note\": \"Single clip returned as-is\"}"
  exit 0
fi

# Download each clip
IDX=0
while IFS= read -r url; do
  url=$(echo "$url" | tr -d ' ')
  [ -z "$url" ] && continue
  CLIP_FILE="$WORKDIR/clip_${IDX}.$FORMAT"
  curl -sL -o "$CLIP_FILE" "$url"
  echo "file '$CLIP_FILE'" >> "$CONCAT_FILE"
  IDX=$((IDX + 1))
done <<< "$URLS"

# Stitch clips
ffmpeg -y -f concat -safe 0 -i "$CONCAT_FILE" -c copy "$OUTPUT_FILE" 2>/dev/null

if [ -f "$OUTPUT_FILE" ]; then
  SIZE=$(stat -f%z "$OUTPUT_FILE" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$OUTPUT_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
  echo "{\"output_url\": \"file://$OUTPUT_FILE\", \"clips_count\": $IDX, \"file_size_bytes\": $SIZE, \"format\": \"$FORMAT\"}"
else
  echo '{"error": "FFmpeg stitching failed - no output file produced"}'
fi
